meaning: n.八个一组的物品,八度音阶
examples: He's singing an octave lower than the rest of us.
Running up to an acid semitonal acciaccatura in both hands, the piano goes over into a sprint of octave-chords and single notes.
It's got only six notes within the octave.
We have moments where we are using the four-to-almost-five-octave range of the choir.
It involves a degree of variation and improvisation in both vocal parts but always ends with two performers singing in unison or an octave apart.
